Facts.text: Pope-elect, the future Gregory the Great, leads a litanic procession through Rome to the church of Santa Maria Maggiore to appease divine anger amid a plague epidemic
Battle at Dan-no-ura: Major sea battle in Japan's Genpei War, Minamoto clan led by Minamoto no Yoshitsune defeats the Taira clan
Muhammad VI ruler of Granada killed with a lance personally by Peter I of Castile, along with 36 followers and his head sent to Muhammad V (or 27 April)
Anti-pope Felix V resigns
German cartographer Martin Waldseemüller is the first to use the name America on his world map "Universalis Cosmographia"
-26) Liege flooded after heavy down pour
Count Maurits' army lands at Cadzand
Battle at Gibraltar: Dutch fleet beats Spanish Portuguese fleet
Shortly before making landfall in America, Captain Edward Maria Wingfield is named President of the Jamestown governing council
Amsterdam Bank of Loan forms
French troops conquer Ypres
Patent granted for thimble
Battle of Almansa; Franco-Spanish forces defeat British and Portuguese
Prince Willem V appointed viceroy of Zealand
"La Marseillaise", later the national anthem of France, is composed by Claude Joseph Rouget de Lisle in Strasbourg
Guillotine first used in France, executes highwayman Nicolas Pelletier
Charles Fremantle arrives in HMS Challenger off the coast of modern-day Western Australia prior to declaring the Swan River Colony for the United Kingdom
Thornton Affair: Open conflict begins over the disputed border of Texas, triggering the Mexican–American War
The Governor General of Canada, Lord Elgin, signs the Rebellion Losses Bill, outraging Montreal's English population and triggering the Montreal Riots.
Paul Julius Reuter sets up carrier-pigeon service, using 40 pigeons to carry stock market prices between Aachen and Brussels
Fraser River Gold Rush begins in British Columbia as miners arrive from California
Ground broken for Suez Canal
Battle of Lavaca, fought in Texas, Union victory (US Civil War)
The Union Army arrives to reinforce Washington, D.C. (US Civil War)
Battle of Marks' Mill, Arkansas (Camden Expedition)
Tokyo opens for foreign trade
Latest date for measurable snow in NYC (3")
Chicago Cubs 1st NL game, beats Louisville 4-0 (1st NL shutout)
250,000 Germans petition to bar foreign Jews from entering Germany
Battle of Hanoi: French forces led by naval captain Henri Rivière capture the Citadel of Hanoi in less than an hour inflicting heavy losses on the Vietnamese defenders
US PresidentBenjamin Harrisonvisits San Francisco
Fight in Central Dance Hall starts fire at Cripple Creek, Colorado
Sidney Jones & Harry Greenbacks musical "The Geisha" premieres at Daly's Theatre, London; runs for 760 performances
Erve Beck hits American League's 1st home run
In last of 9th, Detroit Tigers, trailing by 13-4, score 10 runs to win one of greatest comebacks in baseball (1st game in Detroit)
New York becomes 1st state requiring automobile license plates ($1 fee)
Erwin Harvey becomes 1st Cleveland ballplayer (Cleveland Bronchos) to have six hits in one game
Latest day of 1st-class cricket in an Aust season (NSW v Qld)
Whites win right to vote in South Africa
Magda Julin of Sweden beats teammate Svea Norén for the gold medal in women's singles figure skating at the Antwerp Olympics
Former Persian cossack officer Reza Chan crowns himself Shah Palawi in Tehran
Spain routes 20,000 soldiers to Morocco (uprising Rifkabylen)
Buddy, a German Shepherd, becomes 1st guide dog for a US citizen Morris Frank
Rose Franken's "Another Language" premieres in NYC
NY Yankee Russ Van Atta shuts out Washington Senators 16-0
Philadelphia Phillies' Dick Bartell is 1st MLB player to get 4 consecutive doubles in 9 innings
Beginning of a 3 night bombing blitz on Bath by German Luftwaffe - 417 killed
The Demyansk Shield for German troops in commemoration of Demyansk Pocket instituted
United Negro College Fund incorporates
"Elbe Day" - US and Soviet forces meet at Torgau, Germany on the Elbe River during the invasion of Germany in World War II
45 countries convene UN Conference on Intl Organization in San Francisco
Allied air raid on Surabaja, Java
British troops reach the Grebbe Line, a forward defensive line in the Netherlands
Clandestine Radio 1212, used to hoax Nazi Germany's final transmission
Last Boeing B-17 attack against Nazi Germany
"Exposition Flyer" rammed at Napierville Illinois, killing 48
Christopher Fry's "Phoenix too Frequent" premieres in London
Trial against WW II mayor of Amsterdam Edward Voete begins
Chuck Cooper becomes the 1st African American to be drafted into the NBA (for Boston Celtics)
Republik Maluku Selatan (also known as the Republic of the South Moluccas) declared on Ambon
American Bowling Congress approves use of an automatic pinsetter
German state of Bathe-Wurttemberg forms
British raid Nairobi, Kenya (25,000 Mau Mau suspects arrested)
US performs atmospheric nuclear test above Bikini Island
WDEF TV channel 12 in Chattanooga, TN (CBS) begins broadcasting
1st experimental sodium nuclear reactor operated
Ibrahim Hashim forms Jordanian government
WUHY TV channel 35 in Philadelphia, PA (PBS) begins broadcasting
St Lawrence Seaway linking Atlantic and the Great Lakes opens to shipping
First submerged circumnavigation of Earth is completed by the USS submarine Triton in 60 days and 21 hours
France performs nuclear test at Reggane Proving Grounds Algeria
Mercury-Atlas 3 rocket lifts off with a robot electronic mannequin inside the capsule and goes straight up forcing NASA to destroy it 43 seconds into the mission
Premier Moise Tsjombe of Katanga arrested in Congo
Robert Noyce patents integrated circuit
Cleveland sends Harry Chiti to the Mets for a player to be named later; on June 15, the Mets send Chiti back to Cleveland
Lion & Tiger Veldt at Cleveland Metroparks Zoo is dedicated (Cleveland, Ohio)
US Ranger spacecraft crash lands on Moon
US resumes above ground nuclear testing, at Christmas Island
11th National Film Awards (India): "Shehar Aur Sapna" wins the Golden Lotus
Stanley Cup Final, Maple Leaf Gardens, Toronto, ON: 3-peat for the Toronto Maple Leafs with a 4-0 Game 7 win over Detroit Red Wings for a 4-3 series victory
Drunk driver kills 10 children in Asse, Belgium
Rare daylight meteor observed from New Jersey, US to Quebec, Canada in the early evening
Abortion legalized in Colorado
Britain grants internal self-government to Swaziland
5,400th & last episode of BBC Radio serial "The Dales" (formerly "Mrs Dale's Diary")
Buddha Records releases Melanie's breakthrough single "Lay Down"
Holland-Dozier-Holland's Invictus Records releases Freda Payne's signature single "Band of Gold"
About 200,000 anti-Vietnam War protesters march on Washington, D.C.
The Northern Ireland census is held
US canal rights in Nicaragua and rights to Corn Islands expire
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
Hans-Werner Grosse glides 907.7 miles (1,461 km) in an AS-W-12
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Leo Tindemans forms Belgium government
NFL moves goal posts & adopts sudden-death playoff
Portuguese Prime Minister Marcello Caetano is overthrown in the Carnation revolution, bringing Portugal's authoritarian Estado Novo (New State) government to an end
First Boeing Jetfoil revenue service, Hong Kong to Macau
USSR performs underground nuclear test
West German embassy blown up in Stockholm, Sweden
Cub centerfielder Rick Monday rescues US flag from 2 fans trying to set it on fire
Elections in Vietnam for a National Assembly to reunite the country
India all out for 97 v West Indies
Portugal adopts new constitution
Cincinnati Reds tie record of 12 runs in 5th inning beating Braves 23-9
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
ABC premiere of crime drama "Vega$"
Mascot Phillie Phanatic makes its first appearance
Supreme Court rules pension plans can't require women to pay more
Peace treaty between Israel & Egypt goes into effect
Announcement of US hostage rescue bungle in Iran
Firestone World Bowling Tournament of Champions won by Wayne Webb
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
More than 100 workers are exposed to radiation during repairs of a nuclear power plant in Tsuruga, Japan
Great Britain performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
In accordance with Camp David, Israel completes Sinai withdrawal
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
"Nightline" expands from ½ hour to a full hour
France performs nuclear test at Mururoa Atoll
NASA space probe Pioneer 10 travels beyond Pluto's orbit
Portugal'sMário Soares' Partido Socialista wins parliamentary election
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
West German Parliament rules it is illegal to deny the Holocaust
ETA bomb attacks Madrid killing 5
Mswati III is crowned King of Swaziland, succeeding his father Sobhuza II
American news program "Nightline" goes on location to Jerusalem Israel
John Demjanjuk (Ivan the Terrible), Nazi concentration camp guard, sentenced to death in Jerusalem
NASA launches space vehicle S-211
Penguin Mario Lemuix ties NHL playoff record of 4 goals in 1st period
Hong Kong actress Carina Lau is abducted by Triad members
Hubble space telescope is placed into orbit by space shuttle Discovery
Violeta Chamorro begins a 6-year term as Nicaragua's 1st female president

Boston Herald journalist Lisa Olson brings suit against NFL New England Patriots for sexual harassment while covering team; an out-of-court settlement was reached, the league fines the team, and she transfers to a position in Sydney, Australia
Iraq announces it expects to resume crude oil and product exports by July
Firestone World Bowling Tournament of Champions won by Marc McDowell
NFL Draft: Washington State quarterback Drew Bledsoe first pick by New England Patriots
14 inches of snow falls in Southern California
Bomb attack on taxi stand in Johannesburg, South Africa kills 10
Fishing boat with school children capsizes at Latakia, Syria, 46 killed
King Azlan Shah of Malaysia resigns
Mexican businessman and billionaire Angel Losada kidnapped
Tsutomu Hata elected premier of Japan
"Bring in Da Noise, Bring in Da Funk" opens at Ambassador Theater NYC
Seattle MarinerKen Griffey Jr.hits his 250th HR
107 die in Amagasaki rail crash in Japan
Bulgaria and Romania sign accession treaties to join the European Union
Final piece of the Obelisk of Axum is returned to Ethiopia after being stolen by the invading Italian army in 1937
Boris Yeltsin's funeral, the first to be sanctioned by the Russian Orthodox Church for a head of state since the funeral of Tsar Alexander III in 1894
At least 300 people are killed in the deadliest tornado outbreak in the Southern United States since the 1974 Super Outbreak
The United Kingdom dips back into recession after the economy shrank 0.2% in the first quarter of 2012
38 people are killed in a psychiatric hospital fire in Ramensky, Russia
NFL Draft: Central Michigan offensive tackle Eric Fisher first pick by Kansas City Chiefs
The United Kingdom reopens its embassy in Somalia after 22 years
100 year anniversary of the Gallipoli Peninsula landings in Turkey by ANZAC forces during WWI
7.8-magnitude earthquake near Kathmandu in Nepal, killing 8000, leaving over 100,000 homeless, destroying many historic sites
Danish inventor Peter Madsen found guilty of killing and desecrating body of journalist Kim Wall aboard submarine, sentenced to life imprisonment
Indian religious leader Asaram Bapu is sentenced to life for raping a 16-year old girl
Cyclone Kenneth strikes Mozambique with winds of 220km/h (140mph) killing at least38 people and damaging nearly 35,000 homes
Microsoft becomes the third US firm to be listed with a market worth of 1 trillion, after Apple and Amazon
More than 1,600 civilians were killed in US-led coalition air and land strikes on Raqqa in 2017, according to Amnesty International and monitoring group Airwars
NFL Draft: Oklahoma quarterback Kyler Murray #1 pick by Arizona Cardinals
Thousands march in Khartoum, Sudan calling for civilian rule
First airport for electric flying cars and large drones, built by Urban-Air Port, begins operations in Coventry, England
Japanese M1 spacecraft crashes on the moon, in its attempt to become the first privately-owned spacecraft to land on the Moon
Record April temperatures continue across Thailand, Myanmar, Laos and Vietnam, as Myanmar city of Theinzayet registers record 43C (109.4F)
"Geologic Atlas of the Lunar Globe", highest-ever resolution map of the moon published after 10 years work by The Chinese Academy of Sciences
Popular song lyrics have become simpler and more repetitive, according to a new study that analysed songs from 1970-2020
Battle of Dessau Bridge:Albrecht von Wallensteinat head of Holy Roman Empire forces defeats Danish attempt led by Ernst von Mansfeld's to cross Elbe River (Thirty Years' War)
Last Ming EmperorChongzhenhangs himself from a tree on Jing Mountain, Beijing, rather than be captured by forces of Li Zicheng
English Convention Parliament meets and votes to restoreCharles II
Daniel Defoepublishes "Robinson Crusoe", regarded as the 1st English novel
Elizabeth of Russiacrowns herself Empress in the Dormition Cathedral in Moscow
After the Battle of Forts Jackson and St. Philip, Union forces under Flag OfficerDavid Farragutrequest the surrender New Orleans, LA, despite being largely defenseless armed mobs reuse
Sigmund Freudopens his first neurologist practice at Rathausstrasse 7 in Vienna
US PresidentWoodrow Wilsonis persuaded by Argentina, Brazil, and Chile to accept mediation in the conflict with Mexico
San Remo conference establishes three League of Nations mandates: a French mandate for Syria, and British mandates for Mesopotamia and Palestine with effect to the terms of theBalfour Declaration
Paul von Hindenburgis elected the President of Germany
Giacomo Puccini's opera "Turandot" premieres in Milan
Operation Mercury:Adolf Hitlerorders the conquest of the island of Crete, the first mainly airborne invasion in military history
Lou Theszbeats Whipper Watson in St Louis, to win National Wrestling Association world heavyweight title
Friedrich Durrenmatt's play "Romulus der Grosse (Romulus the Great)" premieres in Basel
Francis CrickandJames Watson's discovery of the double helix structure of DNA is published in "Nature" magazine

Captiol Records releases "In the Wee Small Hours", the ninth studio album by American vocalistFrank Sinatra; collection of melancholy ballads with arrangements by Nelson Riddle was an early example of a concept album and helped popularize the 12-inch format for non-classical recordings
Noël Coward's musical "South Sea Bubble" premieres in London
Belgium government ofGaston Eyskensfalls over the Unitary Law
"NewPhil SilversShow" last airs on CBS-TV
Günter Guillaume, an aide to West German ChancellorWilly Brandt, is exposed as a Stasi spy (East German secret service)
Mário Soares' Socialist Party wins Portugal's first free election since 1925 following the Carnation Revolution of 1974
Oakland Athletics' managerBilly Martinis restrained by umpires from attacking a fan
Seattle Mariners managerMaury Willsis suspended for two games after ordering the Kingdome grounds crew to enlarge the batter's boxes by one foot
Yuri Andropovinvites US schoolgirl Samantha Smith to the Soviet Union
Wayne Gretzkyscores 7 points in a Cup game for the second time (3 goals, 4 assists)
Russia electsBoris YeltsinPresident of the Russian Federation
Columbia Records releasesBruce Springsteen's 14th studio album "We Shall Overcome: The Seeger Sessions", a tribute to folk music pioneerPete Seeger
NFL Draft: Georgia quarterbackMatthew Staffordfirst pick by Detroit Lions
Former US Vice PresidentJoe Bidenannounces his campaign for president via video
93rd Academy Awards: "Nomadland" wins best film, director (Chloé Zhao), and lead actress (Frances McDormand);Anthony Hopkinsbest actor
Charlotte Brontë’s "A Book of Ryhmes" written when she was 13, sells at auction in New York for $1.25 million to Friends of the National Libraries for theBrontëParsonage Museum
Twitter announces a deal to sell itself toElon Muskfor $44 billion
Spanish–American War: The United States declares state of war on Spain effective from 21st April
First landings at Gaba Tepe and Cape Helles on the Gallipoli Peninsula by ANZAC forces during World War I
Soviet forces complete their encirclement of Berlin, cutting off all access points west of the German capital
Bell labs announces the first solar battery made from silicon. It has about 6% efficiency.
Frederick II [the Warlike], Duke of Austria and Styria (1230-1246), born in Wiender Neustadt (d. 1246)
Conrad IV, King of Germany, Jerusalem and Sicily, born in Andria, Sicily (d. 1254)

Princess Mary, Duchess of Gloucester and Edinburgh, born in Buckingham Palace, London (d. 1857)
John Keble, Anglican priest and founder (Oxford Movement), born in Fairford, Gloucestershire, England (d. 1886)

Jean Demoor, Belgian physician and physiologist, born in Etterbeek, Belgium (d. 1941)

John Bevins Moisant, American pioneer aviator, first to cross English Channel with passenger and a cat, killed in New Orleans whose present day airport was originally name for him, born in Kankakee, Illinois (d. 1910)
